title: Venus's Descent
logline: In the shadow of Venus, two titans compete for dominance, each racing to conquer its surface before the planet's toxic atmosphere claims them.
seed_prompt: Two rival megacorporations race to terraform Venus — but only one can survive the process
world_rules: Corporations must adhere to strict environmental regulations on Venus to avoid catastrophic consequences.

acts.summary: The first act introduces the two corporations and their ambitions, setting up the conflict.
The second act delves deeper into the corporate rivalry, revealing internal conflicts and external threats.
The final act climaxes with the decisive battle between the rivals, as both try to claim control over Venus.

chapters.title: Begin your story here
chapters.content:
Begin your story here.

### VENUS'S DESCENT

The city was vast and sprawling under an eternal twilight sky, where towering skyscrapers pierced the dense smog that enveloped it like a thick fog blanket. The ground itself seemed almost nonexistent, but in reality it was solid enough to bear the weight of millions upon millions of tons of dust and debris from every corner of Earth. From high above, one could see how much work had been done; the once lush forests now lay barren, replaced by endless fields of white ash that stretched out endlessly across the horizon. Only those few who lived there were aware of this bleakness below—those lucky souls who chose to stay underground rather than face what awaited beyond the walls of the city they called home. But even among them, some knew better than others just how dire things would become when the clouds finally cleared away and revealed what lay beneath.

It wasn't until many years after the initial collapse of humanity that any trace remained of civilization outside these four massive domes built by Corporation A. They were made of sturdy steel reinforced against the worst weather conditions known to man, but still they could not protect them entirely from disaster. For decades after that terrible night—the day humans realized they had destroyed themselves forever—it was thought impossible that anyone would return from outside the dome. Yet slowly, little by little, people began to find ways through the cracks and crevices created by the crumbling earth and the relentless winds sweeping down from space. Some went mad searching for answers, driven by desperation and hopelessness. Others simply took comfort in knowing that if you didn't die right then, perhaps someday you might survive.

But none of them truly believed they'd be able to escape again without destroying the entire place along with it. It became clear long ago that whatever was happening inside the dome wouldn't stop anytime soon unless something drastic happened—an event so powerful that nothing else in existence could possibly resist it. So instead, most decided to make do with whatever resources they could gather within the confines of their own small living spaces. And though life was hard, there was always someone willing to help another person out, sharing supplies or food amongst the survivors. After such extreme circumstances, compassion never really vanished. People learned to trust one another more quickly than ever before. There came a time too when certain kinds of technology evolved to keep pace with mankind’s need for survival - new innovations that allowed individuals to build bridges between different groups despite being confined to separate territories within the dome. Slowly, society found ways to continue functioning while separated from one another. Technology also provided opportunities for education and entertainment, creating a unique blend of learning and fun during times devoid of normal human interactions.  
In essence, the city of Corus has survived because of the determination of its inhabitants, coupled with advancements in technologies that allow them to thrive despite the harsh realities of their situation. Through sheer grit and ingenuity, they have adapted and adapted well. Despite facing unimaginable odds, the resilience displayed by the citizens shows us that our species isn’t lost—or broken—in the face of adversity.  

Corporation A is currently engaged in a fierce competition with Corporation B. While Corporation A aims to improve lives through technological innovation, offering advanced eco-bricks designed specifically to sustainably transform waste materials into useful products, Corporation B strives primarily towards economic growth through ruthless exploitation of natural resources. Over the last decade, Corporation B has managed to accumulate immense wealth due to successful ventures in mining, oil extraction, and other extractive industries. Its aggressive expansion strategy involves acquiring land rights worldwide, using forced labor practices, and bribing local governments to gain access to raw materials. These actions have led to significant disputes and confrontations between countries and international organizations seeking to enforce sustainable development policies. Moreover, corruption has played a critical role in maintaining profitability by manipulating financial systems and securing tax exemptions, which further fuels its success. This combination of greed and lack of ethical considerations makes Corporation B stand apart in terms of power and influence globally. However, Corporation A views itself as having superior moral principles since it invests heavily in renewable energy solutions and recycling projects that aim to reduce pollution and promote green initiatives. The company's commitment to preserving the environment and fostering community welfare sets it apart from competitors looking solely at profit margins. As tensions rise between the two giants, neither wishes to back down, fearing losing market share, eroding public confidence, and jeopardizing their future prosperity.
